description: v2 Parquet conformance fixtures for the databonk/parquet subpath (ADR-011, bead dataframe-dh9.2). Two layer types: 'parquet_roundtrip' and 'parquet_error'. Round-trip cases define a databonk DataFrame, write it with writeParquet(), read it back with readParquet(), and assert the columns match. The parquet-wasm devDep oracle validates in-profile write output and generates test files for out-of-profile error cases. All supported dtypes are covered (ADR-011 §supported-profile table). Error cases assert that readParquet() throws with a message matching 'error_pattern' as a substring — never a silent wrong result (ADR-011 §Consequences). Column data encoding follows the same conventions as i64.json/temporal.json: i64 values are decimal strings, f64/f32/i32/u32/bool are JSON numbers (0/1 for bool), utf8 are JSON strings or null. date32 is an i32 day count. timestamp is a decimal-string i64 epoch-ms. Null values are represented as JSON null.

cases.name: parquet_roundtrip__f64_with_nulls
cases.note: Basic f64 column with one null value. Maps to Parquet DOUBLE physical type (ADR-011). Nulls round-trip via Parquet definition levels <-> validity bitmap. write_opts default = uncompressed.

cases.name: parquet_roundtrip__f32
cases.note: f32 column maps to Parquet FLOAT physical type (ADR-011). f32 has 24-bit significand; values above 2^24 may round. Null survives definition-level round-trip.

cases.name: parquet_roundtrip__i32
cases.note: i32 column (Parquet INT32 physical). No nulls — tests all-valid path.

cases.name: parquet_roundtrip__u32
cases.note: u32 column maps to Parquet INT32 + INTEGER(bitWidth=32, signed=false) logical annotation (ADR-011). u32 max = 4294967295.

cases.name: parquet_roundtrip__i64_with_nulls
cases.note: i64 column (Parquet INT64 physical). Values above 2^53 require i64 (not f64) to preserve precision. ADR-011.

cases.name: parquet_roundtrip__bool_with_nulls
cases.note: bool column (Parquet BOOLEAN). Null bool is distinct from false. 0=false, 1=true in fixture encoding.

cases.name: parquet_roundtrip__utf8_dict_encoded
cases.note: utf8 column maps to Parquet BYTE_ARRAY + STRING(UTF8), dictionary-encoded (ADR-011, ADR-002). Null utf8 rows must survive the definition-level round-trip.

cases.name: parquet_roundtrip__date32
cases.note: date32 column maps to Parquet INT32 + DATE logical type (ADR-011). Values: 1969-12-31=-1, epoch=0, 2000-02-29=11016. Nulls survive.

cases.name: parquet_roundtrip__timestamp_utc
cases.note: timestamp (no tz) maps to Parquet INT64 + TIMESTAMP(MILLIS, isAdjustedToUTC=true) (ADR-011). The tz metadata (null for UTC) must round-trip: output column has no tz. Epoch ms as decimal strings.

cases.name: parquet_roundtrip__timestamp_with_tz
cases.note: timestamp with tz='America/Chicago': Parquet TIMESTAMP(MILLIS, isAdjustedToUTC=true); the tz string round-trips in logical-type or key_value_metadata (ADR-011). Physical values are UTC ms unchanged.

cases.name: parquet_roundtrip__multi_dtype_frame
cases.note: Multi-column DataFrame covering the primary supported dtypes in one file. Exercises schema discovery, per-column definition levels, and correct column ordering on read. All 5 rows have at least one null in some column.

cases.name: parquet_roundtrip__snappy_compression
cases.note: Snappy is one of the two supported compression codecs (ADR-011). writeParquet with compression='snappy' must produce Parquet bytes that hyparquet can read back correctly. The parquet-wasm oracle also verifies the bytes.

cases.name: parquet_read__gzip_throws
cases.note: GZIP compression is explicitly out-of-profile (ADR-011 §supported-profile). The test runner uses the parquet-wasm oracle to write a Parquet file with GZIP-compressed columns, then calls readParquet() on it and expects a throw with 'unsupported' in the message. Never a silent wrong result.

cases.name: parquet_read__zstd_throws
cases.note: ZSTD compression is out-of-profile. Oracle generates a ZSTD-compressed file; readParquet() must throw.

cases.name: parquet_read__int96_timestamp_throws
cases.note: INT96 timestamp (legacy Hive/Impala format) is explicitly out-of-profile (ADR-011). The oracle writes a Parquet file with an INT96 physical timestamp column; readParquet() must throw naming the unsupported type.

cases.name: parquet_read__decimal_column_throws
cases.note: DECIMAL logical type is out-of-profile (ADR-011). The oracle writes a Parquet file with a DECIMAL column; readParquet() must throw.

cases.name: parquet_read__list_column_throws
cases.note: LIST (nested/repeated) columns are out-of-profile (ADR-011: 'nested/repeated (LIST/MAP/STRUCT) columns'). The oracle writes a Parquet file with a LIST<INT32> column; readParquet() must throw.

cases.name: parquet_write__invalid_codec_throws
cases.note: writeParquet() with an unsupported compression codec string must throw a clear error. The test runner calls writeParquet(df, {compression: 'gzip'}) and asserts the throw. ADR-011 §API-shape: only 'snappy' and 'uncompressed' are accepted by writeParquet.
